snippet: Matrix problems appear at every FAANG company, spiral traversal, number of islands, rotting oranges (multi-source BFS), word search, and the 0/1 matrix distance problem. Master the two core approaches: DFS/BFS for connectivity and spread, and simulation with direction arrays for traversal order problems.

title: Monotonic Stack and Deque: Next Greater Element in O(n)
snippet: The single most underused O(n) pattern in coding interviews. Learn exactly when to use a monotonic stack vs. deque, why storing indices beats storing values, and how to solve histogram rectangle and sliding window maximum problems that stump most candidates.

title: Parallel & Concurrent Algorithms: Fork-Join, Work Stealing, and Amdahl's Law
snippet: 45 minParallel & Concurrent Algorithms: Fork-Join, Work Stealing, and Amdahl's LawInterview-ready foundations beyond serial Big-O: identify embarrassingly parallel loops, derive speedups with Amdahl's law, understand Java ForkJoinPool work-stealing, parallel prefix scan hierarchy, and why parallel quicksort rarely beats tuned serial sorts, with complexity pitfalls candidates miss.

title: The 8 Core DSA Patterns
snippet: Master the 8 pattern families that cover 98% of coding interview questions at top tech companies. Pattern recognition is the single highest-leverage skill for coding interviews.

title: Prefix Sums & Hashing: Subarray Problems in O(n)
snippet: Two of the most frequently tested DSA patterns: prefix sums convert subarray queries from O(n) to O(1), and hash maps eliminate the inner loop in most two-pass problems. Covers subarray sum equals K, two-sum variants, longest subarray with constraint, and the combined prefix-sum + hash technique.

title: Sliding Window: Fixed and Variable Window Patterns
snippet: Master the sliding window pattern for contiguous subarray and substring problems. Covers fixed-size and variable-size windows, the expand-shrink template, and the key O(n) insight that most candidates miss until they understand why left never moves backward.

title: Two Pointers: Converging, Fast/Slow, and Floyd's Cycle Detection
snippet: Three distinct two-pointer variants with precise invariants, proven correctness, and production-quality implementations. Covers converging pointers for sorted arrays, fast/slow for duplicates and linked list middle, and Floyd's cycle detection with the phase 2 math that most candidates get wrong.

title: Binary Search Patterns: 3 Templates and Bisect-on-Answer
snippet: Master all three binary search templates, exact match, predicate search, and bisect-on-answer. Covers rotated arrays, peak finding, and the hardest LC variant: median of two sorted arrays in O(log min(m,n)).

title: Sorting Algorithms: Merge Sort, Quickselect, and Custom Comparators
snippet: Sorting for FAANG interviews: when to sort, when to use counting/radix sort, how merge sort counts inversions, and how Quickselect finds Kth largest in O(n) average. Covers the comparison-sort lower bound and Python custom comparators.

title: Graph Algorithms: BFS, DFS & Topological Sort
snippet: Master graph traversal with clear templates for BFS and DFS, cycle detection, topological sort, and shortest path algorithms. Covers both implicit and explicit graph problems.

title: Heaps & Priority Queues: Top-K, Merge K Lists, and Two-Heap Patterns
snippet: Master the three heap interview patterns: top-K elements in O(n log k), merging K sorted streams in O(N log k), and the two-heap median trick. Includes Python heapq API and lazy deletion for complex problems.

title: Binary Trees & BST: Traversals, LCA, and Classic Patterns
snippet: Master binary tree traversals (DFS, BFS, iterative), BST operations with full complexity analysis, Lowest Common Ancestor, and serialize/deserialize. Covers the recursive template that solves 80% of tree interview problems.

title: Tries (Prefix Trees): Autocomplete, Word Search, and Wildcard Matching
snippet: Build production-ready tries for O(L) prefix operations, solve Word Search II with backtracking pruning, and implement wildcard matching. Covers the prefix-count optimization used in autocomplete systems.

title: Union-Find (DSU): Dynamic Connectivity in Near-Constant Time
snippet: Master Disjoint Set Union with path compression and union by rank for O(α(n)) per operation. Covers connected components, cycle detection, accounts merge, and when DSU outperforms BFS/DFS for graph problems.

title: Knapsack DP: 0/1, Unbounded, and Subset Sum Variants
snippet: Master all knapsack variants by understanding one mental model: dp[i][w] = best value with first i items and capacity w. Covers 0/1 knapsack, unbounded, bounded, Partition Equal Subset Sum, Coin Change, Target Sum, and the critical forward-vs-reverse iteration insight that distinguishes 0/1 from unbounded.

title: DP on Strings: LCS, Edit Distance, and Regex Matching
snippet: Master the universal 2D string DP template that unlocks LCS, Edit Distance, Longest Palindromic Subsequence, and Regex Matching. Covers state definitions, space optimization from O(nm) to O(min(n,m)), and the five string DP archetypes that appear most in FAANG interviews.

title: Dynamic Programming: From Recursion to Optimization
snippet: A systematic approach to solving DP problems: from recognizing when to use DP, to formulating states, to writing clean bottom-up solutions. Covers 1D, 2D, and interval DP patterns.

title: Game Theory for Coding Interviews: Minimax, Alpha-Beta, Nim, and DP vs Search
snippet: 38 minGame Theory for Coding Interviews: Minimax, Alpha-Beta, Nim, and DP vs SearchAlgorithms and LeetCode interview prep on two-player games: minimax, alpha-beta pruning (Wikipedia), Nim XOR, stone-game interval DP, and when memoized DP beats exponential O(b^d) search. BOTIC execution, branching-factor analysis, typed Python alpha-beta.

title: String Pattern Matching: KMP, Rabin-Karp, and Z-Algorithm
snippet: 22 minString Pattern Matching: KMP, Rabin-Karp, and Z-AlgorithmThe three string-matching algorithms that transform O(nm) brute force into O(n+m). KMP's failure function, Rabin-Karp's rolling hash, and the Z-array, with the non-obvious problems they unlock and why each is asked at Google, Amazon, and Databricks.

title: Advanced Graph Algorithms: Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, Topological Sort, and SCC
snippet: 28 minAdvanced Graph Algorithms: Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, Topological Sort, and SCCThe four graph algorithm families every senior engineer must know cold: shortest paths with and without negative weights, topological ordering for dependency graphs, and strongly connected components. With production complexity analysis and the precise failure modes each algorithm has.

title: Segment Trees and Fenwick Trees: Range Queries and Point Updates
snippet: 24 minSegment Trees and Fenwick Trees: Range Queries and Point UpdatesMaster the two most powerful range query data structures used in hard-level interviews at Google, Amazon, and competitive programming. When to use each, how to build them, and the lazy propagation technique that handles range updates in O(log n).

title: Back-of-Envelope Estimation: From DAU to Architecture Decisions
snippet: Back-of-envelope estimation is the first gate in every system design interview — and the most misunderstood. This guide reframes it as a constraint-finding tool: three-step drill (traffic → capacity → storage), 15 critical latency and throughput benchmarks, and worked examples at Twitter, WhatsApp, and YouTube scale.

title: Numbers Every Engineer Should Know: Latency, Throughput, and Storage at a Glance
snippet: The canonical reference card for system design interviews: latency hierarchy from L1 cache to cross-region RTT, per-component throughput benchmarks, storage unit anchors, and connection limits. Internalize these 30 numbers and you will never draw an architecture that cannot handle its own load.

title: Design a Distributed ID Generator (Snowflake / UUID / Segment-Based)
snippet: 38 minDesign a Distributed ID Generator (Snowflake / UUID / Segment-Based)Deep-dive into distributed unique ID generation: Twitter Snowflake bit layout, UUID v4 vs v7 B-tree implications, Instagram's Postgres segment approach, clock skew failure modes, and the monotonicity vs randomness tradeoff every FAANG infrastructure interview tests.

title: Design a Gaming Leaderboard (Real-Time Sorted Sets at Scale)
snippet: Design a global real-time leaderboard for a gaming platform: Redis Sorted Sets for ranking, top-K queries, percentile rank lookups, anti-cheat patterns, sliding windows for tournament leaderboards, segmented leaderboards by region/skill, and the consistency vs latency tradeoffs at billions-of-events scale.

title: Design Pastebin (The Canonical Back-of-Envelope Question)
snippet: Design a Pastebin-style text snippet sharing service end-to-end: back-of-envelope estimation, ID generation strategies, hot vs cold storage tiering, expiration policies, syntax highlighting, abuse detection, and the architectural decisions that look simple but encode every fundamental skill — the perfect intermediate-level system design question.

title: Design a URL Shortener (TinyURL)
snippet: End-to-end design of a URL shortening service handling 10 billion stored URLs, 100K redirects/sec, and sub-5ms p99 redirect latency. Covers ID generation, read-heavy caching, multi-region replication, and async analytics.

title: HLD Interview Framework: CIRCLE Method
snippet: A repeatable 6-step framework for HLD interviews with timing guidance, back-of-envelope estimation references, and the exact questions to ask at each step.

title: How to Approach an HLD System Design Interview
snippet: The pre-game mindset, signal management, and communication strategy for HLD interviews. Covers what interviewers evaluate, time budgeting, recovery patterns, and the failure modes that cause strong engineers to underperform on system design interviews.

title: How to Design at HLD: From Blank Whiteboard to Defensible Architecture
snippet: 35 minHow to Design at HLD: From Blank Whiteboard to Defensible ArchitectureThe mechanical playbook for HLD interview execution. Covers the API-first design rule, capacity-driven technology selection, the five-layer reference architecture, sharding decision tree, caching strategy, and the patterns FAANG candidates use to deliver consistently strong system designs.

title: Backend Networking: VPCs, Subnets, Routing, and Private Connectivity
snippet: 52 minBackend Networking: VPCs, Subnets, Routing, and Private ConnectivityInterview-ready cloud networking: CIDR math and usable IPs, public vs private subnets and NAT, security groups vs NACLs, VPC endpoints vs PrivateLink, Transit Gateway vs peering blast radius, DNS patterns, and hybrid connectivity — grounded in AWS vocabulary with GCP/Azure parallels.

title: Bloom Filters: Probabilistic Set Membership for System Design
snippet: Bloom filters are a core system design primitive: eliminate expensive lookups with guaranteed negative responses. Learn the math behind sizing (optimal k hash functions, false-positive rate formula), when to use vs. avoid them, and how Cassandra, BigTable, Chrome, and Akamai use them in distributed systems.

title: Caching: Strategy, Redis Internals & Distributed Patterns
snippet: Caching is the most tested topic in HLD interviews. Master cache write strategies, Redis data structures and their time complexities, distributed cache topology, the cache invalidation problem, and how to design multi-level caching architectures for real systems.

title: CDN: Edge Caching, Push vs Pull, and Invalidation at Global Scale
snippet: CDNs cut global latency from hundreds of milliseconds to tens, offload 80-99% of origin bandwidth, and shield origins from bursty traffic. Master push vs pull, cache hierarchy, invalidation, and the failure modes that break CDN-backed systems.

title: Cloud Services Architecture for System Design Interviews
snippet: 46 minCloud Services Architecture for System Design InterviewsHow to choose AWS, GCP, and Azure managed services in system design interviews with clear tradeoffs. Covers compute, storage, messaging, networking, identity, and serverless-vs-container decisions tied to latency, reliability, and cost.

title: Distributed Queue Design: Ordering, Retries, and Throughput
snippet: 27 minDistributed Queue Design: Ordering, Retries, and ThroughputDesign production-grade distributed message queue systems covering Kafka vs SQS vs RabbitMQ tradeoffs, delivery semantics, consumer group patterns, backpressure, and dead-letter queues. The system design interview's most-tested async primitive — master it to handle any event-driven architecture question.

title: Load Balancer Design: L4/L7 Routing, Health Checks, and Failover
snippet: Design scalable load balancing for modern distributed systems. Covers L4 vs L7 tradeoffs, routing algorithms (round-robin, least-connections, P2C, consistent hashing), health check design, connection draining, sticky sessions, and global load balancing with GeoDNS and Anycast. Builds the mental model interviewers use to assess system design maturity.

title: Rate Limiter Design: Token Bucket, Sliding Window, and Distributed Enforcement
snippet: Design distributed rate limiters for APIs and gateways. Covers all five algorithm tradeoffs (token bucket, leaky bucket, fixed window, sliding window log, sliding window counter), Redis data structure choices, hot-key mitigation, race conditions, and multi-region consistency. One of the most frequently asked HLD fundamentals at FAANG.

title: Real-Time Protocols: WebSockets vs SSE vs Long Polling vs HTTP/2 Push
snippet: The system design decision guide for real-time communication: WebSockets for bidirectional low-latency, SSE for server-push streams, long polling for legacy-compatible fallback, HTTP/2 Server Push for preloading. Covers connection scaling, Redis pub/sub fan-out, load balancer gotchas, and the WebTransport horizon.

title: Design an Ad Auction / Real-Time Bidding (RTB) Platform
snippet: 55 minDesign an Ad Auction / Real-Time Bidding (RTB) PlatformBuild an end-to-end real-time bidding platform: auction mechanics (first-price vs second-price/Vickrey-GSP), budget pacing algorithms, sub-100ms bid response latency, and the distributed budget enforcement problem that separates novice from senior ad-tech engineers.

title: Design an Ad Click Aggregation System (Stream Processing + Exactly-Once)
snippet: 49 minDesign an Ad Click Aggregation System (Stream Processing + Exactly-Once)Real-time ad click ingestion, deduplication, and aggregation: Kafka + Flink architecture, watermarks for late events, exactly-once semantics via transactional sinks, click fraud detection, hot-key advertiser handling, and the lambda vs kappa architecture decision for billing-grade accuracy.

title: Design a Chat System (WhatsApp)
snippet: End-to-end design of a real-time encrypted messaging platform serving 2 billion users and 100 billion messages per day. Covers WebSocket connection management, the Signal Protocol for E2EE, store-and-forward offline delivery, and group message fan-out.

title: Design a Coding Platform (LeetCode / HackerRank at Scale)
snippet: Design LeetCode at scale: untrusted code execution in gVisor/Firecracker sandboxes, distributed judge queue with priority lanes, cgroup resource limits, real-time submission feedback via SSE and Redis pub/sub, and contest leaderboards via Redis sorted sets — including the security depth that makes sandboxing the hardest part.

title: Design Google Docs (Real-Time Collaborative Editing)
snippet: 59 minDesign Google Docs (Real-Time Collaborative Editing)System design deep-dive for real-time collaborative editing at 1B+ scale. Covers OT vs CRDTs, why Google Docs uses OT with a centralized server, WebSocket sync, offline editing with Yjs, and the tombstone problem that limits CRDT scalability.

title: Design a Digital Wallet (Venmo / Cash App / PayPal Internals)
snippet: 51 minDesign a Digital Wallet (Venmo / Cash App / PayPal Internals)End-to-end design of a P2P digital wallet at scale: double-entry ledger for stored balances, ACH/bank rail integration, instant peer-to-peer transfers, fraud and AML/KYC screening, multi-currency, regulatory deposit account structuring (FBO accounts), and the consistency model differences from a card payment processor.

title: Design a Distributed Cache (Memcached / Redis Cluster Internals)
snippet: 48 minDesign a Distributed Cache (Memcached / Redis Cluster Internals)Build the internals of a distributed in-memory cache: consistent hashing with virtual nodes, gossip-based cluster membership, hot key mitigation, eviction policies (LRU/LFU/W-TinyLFU), replication for durability, and the cache stampede / thundering herd problem with request coalescing.

title: Design a Distributed File System (GFS / HDFS / Colossus Internals)
snippet: 50 minDesign a Distributed File System (GFS / HDFS / Colossus Internals)Deep-dive into Google File System, HDFS, and Colossus internals: master/NameNode design, chunk replication pipelines, fault tolerance, HDFS Federation to break the single-NameNode bottleneck, and how Colossus eliminates the single master entirely via Bigtable-backed metadata.

title: Design an Email Service at Gmail Scale
snippet: 52 minDesign an Email Service at Gmail ScaleArchitect a Gmail-scale email service: SMTP/IMAP gateway, distributed mailbox storage, full-text search index, spam and phishing detection, attachment storage, threading via References headers, push notification, and the consistency tradeoffs between sent, delivered, and read states.

title: Design a Feature Flag Platform (SSE, Sticky Rollouts, Kill Switches)
snippet: 40 minDesign a Feature Flag Platform (SSE, Sticky Rollouts, Kill Switches)Real-time flags: deterministic hash bucketing, rule precedence, SSE brokers to SDKs, ETag snapshots, control vs data plane split, and priority kill-switch fan-out — plus why Redis-per-request fails and how to document staleness SLAs for interviews.

title: Design a File Storage and Sync System (Dropbox / Google Drive)
snippet: 70 minDesign a File Storage and Sync System (Dropbox / Google Drive)End-to-end system design for a Dropbox-scale file storage and sync platform serving 500M users and 2.5EB of data. Covers content-addressable chunking, metadata-blob separation, the sync protocol, shared-folder consistency, version history, and the garbage-collection edge cases that interviewers target.

title: Food Delivery Platform: DoorDash/Uber Eats System Design
snippet: 45 minFood Delivery Platform: DoorDash/Uber Eats System DesignSystem design of a food delivery platform at DoorDash/Uber Eats scale. Covers ML-driven dasher dispatch, two-stage ETA prediction, distributed systems patterns including saga and idempotency, and a Kafka + Redis Geo location pipeline for 5M orders/day.

title: Design Google Maps (Routing, ETA & Map Tile Serving)
snippet: 61 minDesign Google Maps (Routing, ETA & Map Tile Serving)Deep-dive system design for Google Maps covering map tile serving at CDN scale, Contraction Hierarchies-based routing on a 36M-node road graph, and real-time ETA prediction from 100M GPS probe devices — with production numbers, algorithm tradeoffs, and what senior engineers get wrong.

title: Design a Hotel Booking System (Booking.com at Scale)
snippet: System design deep-dive for a hotel reservation platform at Booking.com scale — 28M listings, 1.5M room-nights/day — covering date-range inventory, overbooking buffers, Redis temporary holds, the search-vs-booking data model split, and thundering herd prevention for peak-load dates.

title: Design Instagram (Photo Sharing at Billion-User Scale)
snippet: 50 minDesign Instagram (Photo Sharing at Billion-User Scale)End-to-end system design for an Instagram-style photo sharing service: blob upload pipeline with chunked resumable uploads, image transcoding fan-out, hybrid push/pull feed delivery for celebrity hot-keys, Cassandra-backed timeline materialization, CDN edge caching, and back-of-envelope numbers grounding the architecture.

title: Design a Distributed Job Scheduler (Celery / Temporal Internals)
snippet: 51 minDesign a Distributed Job Scheduler (Celery / Temporal Internals)System design deep-dive for a distributed job scheduler: priority queues, exactly-once execution, durable retries, cron scheduling, fan-out/fan-in, Temporal-style durable workflow execution, worker fleet management, and at-most-once vs at-least-once consistency tradeoffs.

title: Design a Key-Value Store (LSM Tree / RocksDB / Bigtable Internals)
snippet: 52 minDesign a Key-Value Store (LSM Tree / RocksDB / Bigtable Internals)Build a production key-value store from first principles: LSM tree write path, SSTable compaction strategies, Bloom filters for read optimization, and the architectural tradeoffs that separate RocksDB from Bigtable from DynamoDB. The question that distinguishes engineers who have studied storage engines from those who have only used them.

title: Design a Lakehouse Architecture (Delta Lake / Iceberg / Hudi)
snippet: 50 minDesign a Lakehouse Architecture (Delta Lake / Iceberg / Hudi)Deep-dive into ACID transactions on object storage: Delta Lake's JSON transaction log, Apache Iceberg's manifest tree, Hudi's Copy-on-Write vs Merge-on-Read modes, schema evolution, time travel, compaction, and the engineering tradeoffs that make Databricks, Netflix, and Uber choose different open table formats.

title: Design a Metrics Monitoring & Alerting System (Prometheus / Datadog Internals)
snippet: 47 minDesign a Metrics Monitoring & Alerting System (Prometheus / Datadog Internals)Architect a production-scale metrics platform: time-series ingestion at 10M points/sec, push vs pull collection, downsampling and retention tiers, Gorilla compression, alert evaluation, query engine for aggregations, and high-cardinality label explosion mitigation.

title: Design a News Aggregator (Reddit / Hacker News at Scale)
snippet: Design Reddit at scale: subreddit feeds with vote aggregation, Hot/Rising/New ranking algorithms (Wilson score, HN gravity), comment threading via materialized paths, and sub-100ms feed loads for 400M monthly users using pre-computed Redis sorted sets and Kafka-backed vote streams.

title: News Feed System Design: Fan-Out on Write vs Read, Hybrid Strategies
snippet: The fan-out problem is the central challenge behind every social feed — Twitter, Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn. Master fan-out on write (push), fan-out on read (pull), the hybrid approach for celebrity accounts, feed storage in Redis, Cassandra schema design, and how feed ranking layers on top of chronological ordering.

title: Design a Multi-Channel Notification Service at Scale
snippet: End-to-end design of a notification platform delivering 1B notifications/day across push, email, and SMS. Covers the fan-out problem for broadcast sends, per-provider rate limiting, idempotency, retries, and compliance (GDPR/CAN-SPAM/TCPA) — the system design topics interviewers actually grill on.

title: Design an Object Storage System (S3 Internals)
snippet: 51 minDesign an Object Storage System (S3 Internals)Architect an Amazon S3-style object store: 11 nines durability, erasure coding vs replication, multipart upload, eventual to strong consistency, partition tolerance, versioning, lifecycle policies, and the storage-engine internals that make exabyte scale economical.

title: Design an Online Auction System (eBay-scale Real-Time Bidding)
snippet: Design an online auction system at eBay scale: bid contention via per-auction Kafka queue serialization, real-time bid broadcasts via WebSocket, proxy bidding logic, anti-sniping extension windows, and an auction state machine with time-bounded finalization across 1M+ concurrent auctions.

title: Design a Payment System (Stripe-style Processor)
snippet: 47 minDesign a Payment System (Stripe-style Processor)System design deep-dive for a merchant payment processor handling 10K TPS peak, sub-500ms p99 latency, PCI DSS compliance, idempotent charges, double-entry ledger accounting, fraud scoring, and exactly-once distributed systems semantics under partial failure.

title: Design Proximity Services (Yelp / Google Places)
snippet: 67 minDesign Proximity Services (Yelp / Google Places)System design deep-dive for a geo-search platform handling 200M+ monthly users, sub-100ms nearby business search across 50M+ geo-indexed points, geohash/S2/H3 indexing tradeoffs, read/write split at 100:1, sharding by geohash prefix, and ranking by distance, rating, and personalization signals.

title: Design a Real-Time Analytics Pipeline (Lambda / Kappa / Flink)
snippet: 52 minDesign a Real-Time Analytics Pipeline (Lambda / Kappa / Flink)End-to-end design of a production-grade real-time analytics pipeline: Lambda vs Kappa architecture, stream processing with Apache Flink and Kafka Streams, watermarks and exactly-once semantics, OLAP serving with Druid/ClickHouse/Pinot, and backpressure handling at millions of events per second.

title: Design Uber (Ride-Sharing Platform)
snippet: 29 minDesign Uber (Ride-Sharing Platform)End-to-end design of a real-time ride-matching platform handling millions of simultaneous GPS location updates, sub-10-second driver matching, dynamic surge pricing, and global trip management across 70 countries.

title: Design Search Autocomplete / Typeahead (Google-scale)
snippet: 52 minDesign Search Autocomplete / Typeahead (Google-scale)System design deep-dive for a search typeahead service delivering sub-100ms top-K suggestions at 10B queries/day using sharded tries, precomputed top-K at each node, multi-tier caching, real-time trending pipelines, distributed systems scalability, and personalization.

title: Design Secrets Management: Vault-Style Leases, Dynamic DB Creds, Transit
snippet: 42 minDesign Secrets Management: Vault-Style Leases, Dynamic DB Creds, TransitOrg-wide secrets done right: encrypted barrier, Shamir unseal, dynamic Postgres users with TTL, Vault Transit-style crypto, sidecar renewal, tamper-evident audit, and AWS Secrets Manager tradeoffs — plus lease vs pool alignment and operator blast radius for staff interviews.

title: Design a Social Graph Service (Friend-of-Friend at 1B+ Nodes)
snippet: 48 minDesign a Social Graph Service (Friend-of-Friend at 1B+ Nodes)Graph storage, sharding, BFS at billion-node scale — the core infrastructure behind Facebook, LinkedIn, and Twitter. Covers adjacency-list sharding, TAO (Facebook's graph store), degrees-of-separation traversal, and friend recommendations via Jaccard similarity, with production numbers from systems that serve trillions of edges.

title: Design a Stock Exchange (Order Book & Matching Engine)
snippet: 54 minDesign a Stock Exchange (Order Book & Matching Engine)System design for a stock exchange at NYSE/NASDAQ scale: 10B+ messages/day, sub-microsecond matching via LMAX Disruptor and FPGA, price-time priority order book, UDP multicast for market data, and why the matching engine is intentionally single-threaded for determinism and audit compliance.

title: Design an Event Ticketing System (BookMyShow / Ticketmaster)
snippet: 54 minDesign an Event Ticketing System (BookMyShow / Ticketmaster)System design deep-dive for a flash-sale ticketing platform at Ticketmaster scale — 600K+ concurrent users competing for 50K seats in seconds — covering seat reservation with TTL, oversell prevention via optimistic locking, virtual waiting rooms, WebSocket availability overlays, and idempotent payment flows.

title: Design Twitter/X Feed (News Feed)
snippet: 25 minDesign Twitter/X Feed (News Feed)End-to-end design of a social media news feed serving 200 million daily active users. The central design challenge is the fan-out problem: how to show a user their personalized timeline of tweets from all the accounts they follow, with < 200ms latency, at 180K reads/sec.

title: Video Conferencing Platform: Zoom/Google Meet System Design
snippet: Design a real-time video conferencing system supporting 300M daily participants and 1000-person meetings. Covers WebRTC signaling, SFU vs MCU vs P2P routing, NAT traversal with STUN/TURN, adaptive bitrate via simulcast, and cloud recording — the archetype interviewers use to test real-time media engineering depth.

title: Design Netflix (Video Streaming)
snippet: 30 minDesign Netflix (Video Streaming)End-to-end design of a global video streaming platform serving 250M subscribers across 190 countries, handling 25 Tbps of peak traffic. Covers video ingestion and encoding pipelines, the Open Connect CDN, adaptive bitrate streaming, and recommendation at scale.

title: Design a Web Crawler at Google Scale
snippet: 56 minDesign a Web Crawler at Google ScaleEnd-to-end system design for a distributed web crawler at Google/Bing scale: 15B+ pages, ~6B refreshed daily, covering URL frontier design, OPIC-based priority scoring, politeness enforcement, SimHash near-duplicate detection, distributed sharding, and freshness-vs-depth tradeoffs most resources skip entirely.

title: 10 Architectural Patterns Behind Every Distributed System
snippet: 10 reusable patterns behind every system design interview: Read Scaling, Write Scaling, Real-Time Delivery, Async Work, Contention Management, Large Objects, Distributed Consistency, Service Communication, Data Partitioning, and Fault Isolation. Recognize the pattern first — the solution follows.

title: API Gateway Design: Auth, Rate Limiting, Routing, and BFF Patterns
snippet: Production API gateway architecture covering Kong, Envoy, AWS API Gateway, and BFF patterns. Where to terminate TLS, validate JWTs, enforce rate limits, aggregate requests — and how to avoid turning the gateway into a distributed monolith.

title: Authentication & Authorization: JWT, OAuth 2.0, OIDC & Zanzibar ReBAC
snippet: Deep-dive on modern auth systems: AuthN vs AuthZ, session vs JWT tradeoffs, OAuth 2.0 flows (Authorization Code + PKCE, Client Credentials, Device), OIDC identity tokens, RBAC vs ABAC vs Google Zanzibar ReBAC, JWT revocation, key rotation, and WebAuthn passkeys for FAANG system design interviews.

title: Consensus Protocols: Raft vs Paxos, Leader Election & Log Replication
snippet: 25 minConsensus Protocols: Raft vs Paxos, Leader Election & Log ReplicationDistributed consensus is what makes ZooKeeper, etcd, and CockroachDB correct. Master Raft's three-phase algorithm (leader election → log replication → commit), why Raft is simpler than Paxos, the split-brain scenario, Byzantine fault tolerance, and where consensus appears in real systems — Kafka, Kubernetes, and Google Spanner.

title: Consistency Models: Linearizability, Causal, CRDTs, CAP & Strong vs Eventual
snippet: Consistency models define what values a distributed read can return after a write. Master linearizability (strong consistency), sequential consistency, causal consistency, eventual consistency, and read-your-writes — with practical implications for database selection, microservice design, and the CAP theorem's real-world limitations.

title: Containers and Kubernetes for System Design Interviews
snippet: 42 minContainers and Kubernetes for System Design InterviewsA production-focused guide to Docker and Kubernetes for backend system design interviews. Covers pod scheduling, Deployment vs StatefulSet decisions, autoscaling, service mesh tradeoffs, and failure handling with concrete latency and reliability constraints.

title: Data Mesh Architecture: Domain-Oriented Data Ownership at Scale
snippet: 35 minData Mesh Architecture: Domain-Oriented Data Ownership at ScaleA system design guide to Data Mesh: domain ownership, data product contracts, federated governance with GDPR enforcement, and self-serve platform design. Covers implementations at JPMorgan and Zalando (Databricks + Unity Catalog), and explains when decentralized ownership fixes the data bottleneck—and when it doesn't.

title: Data Partitioning: Consistent Hashing, Range Sharding & Hotspots
snippet: 25 minData Partitioning: Consistent Hashing, Range Sharding & HotspotsSharding is what makes databases scale beyond a single machine. Master horizontal vs vertical partitioning, range sharding, hash sharding, consistent hashing with virtual nodes, hotspot detection, and resharding strategies — with real numbers from Cassandra, DynamoDB, and YouTube's architecture.

title: Data Warehouse Architecture: Columnar Storage, MPP, and Lakehouse Tradeoffs
snippet: 39 minData Warehouse Architecture: Columnar Storage, MPP, and Lakehouse TradeoffsA practical system design guide to modern warehouse architecture using Snowflake, BigQuery, and Redshift patterns. Covers columnar storage internals, MPP execution, partitioning and clustering strategy, and warehouse-vs-lakehouse design choices.

title: Databases: Sharding, Indexing & Replication
snippet: Database engineering for large-scale systems — the most tested HLD sub-topic after caching. Covers B-Tree and LSM-Tree storage engines, indexing strategies (covering, composite, partial), sharding strategies and hotspot handling, replication (sync vs async, leader election), and how to choose between SQL and NoSQL in system design interviews.

title: Distributed Locks: Redlock, ZooKeeper, Fencing Tokens & Exactly-Once Guarantees
snippet: 24 minDistributed Locks: Redlock, ZooKeeper, Fencing Tokens & Exactly-Once GuaranteesDistributed locks are the mechanism behind inventory reservations, payment deduplication, and leader election. Master Redis SET NX EX, the Redlock algorithm and its controversy, ZooKeeper ephemeral znodes, fencing tokens for safe expiry, and when distributed locks are the wrong tool entirely.

title: Distributed Systems Patterns for System Design Interviews
snippet: 27 minDistributed Systems Patterns for System Design InterviewsThe 8 core distributed systems patterns every senior engineer must know: consistent hashing, CAP theorem, saga pattern, CQRS, event sourcing, two-phase commit, gossip protocol, and leader election.

title: Distributed Transactions: 2PC, Saga Pattern, and Compensating Transactions
snippet: 23 minDistributed Transactions: 2PC, Saga Pattern, and Compensating TransactionsDistributed transactions coordinate state changes across multiple services or databases. Two-Phase Commit (2PC) provides strong consistency but sacrifices availability. The Saga pattern achieves eventual consistency through compensating transactions — the approach used by Uber, Amazon, and Stripe for multi-step business workflows. This guide covers both models, their failure modes, and when each applies.

title: Event Sourcing and CQRS: Audit Logs, Temporal Queries, and Read/Write Separation
snippet: 55 minEvent Sourcing and CQRS: Audit Logs, Temporal Queries, and Read/Write SeparationEvent sourcing stores state as an immutable event log rather than mutable rows. CQRS separates write and read models for scalability in distributed systems. Together they power audit logs, temporal queries, and high-read systems at Stripe, Uber, LinkedIn, and Microsoft. Covers sharding strategies, snapshot patterns, schema evolution, projection rebuilds, and the operational challenges most candidates miss.

title: Geohashing and Spatial Indexing: Quad-Trees, S2 Cells, and BKD Trees
snippet: Spatial indexing for system design interviews: geohash Z-order encoding, Google S2 Hilbert-curve cells, quad-trees for dynamic points, and BKD trees in Elasticsearch. Decision framework for choosing between them — covering Uber, Yelp, Foursquare, and Redis production patterns for proximity search.

title: Idempotency Patterns: Safe Retries, Exactly-Once Semantics, and Fencing Tokens
snippet: Idempotency is the difference between a payment processed once and a payment charged three times during a retry storm. A foundational distributed systems pattern: idempotency keys, exactly-once myth vs. effective-once reality, saga compensation, fencing tokens, and how Stripe, Kafka, and Temporal implement safe retries.

title: Message Queues & Streaming: Kafka, Delivery Semantics, and Consumer Groups
snippet: 48 minMessage Queues & Streaming: Kafka, Delivery Semantics, and Consumer GroupsAsync messaging is the backbone of every scalable architecture. Master the queue-vs-log distinction, Kafka partitioning and consumer groups, delivery guarantees, ordering semantics, and the production failure modes that separate staff engineers from the crowd.

title: Microservices: Decomposition, Service Mesh, Circuit Breakers & Saga
snippet: 26 minMicroservices: Decomposition, Service Mesh, Circuit Breakers & SagaWhen and how to decompose a monolith into microservices — and the distributed systems complexity that follows. Covers domain-driven decomposition, the strangler fig migration pattern, service discovery, API gateway, circuit breakers (Hystrix/Resilience4j), service mesh (Istio), and the saga pattern for distributed transactions.

title: Multi-Region Architecture: Active-Active, Active-Passive, and Global Consistency
snippet: Multi-region deployment forces every distributed systems tradeoff into the open: who resolves write conflicts, how much data loss is acceptable, and whether data can cross borders. Master active-passive, active-active, and cell-based architectures with production examples from Stripe, Netflix, and Google Spanner.

title: Observability: Metrics, Distributed Tracing, Structured Logging & SLO Design
snippet: 26 minObservability: Metrics, Distributed Tracing, Structured Logging & SLO DesignObservability is what separates systems that are operated from systems that are debugged by guessing. Master the three pillars (metrics, logs, traces), SLI/SLO/SLA design, error budgets, structured logging, OpenTelemetry for distributed tracing, and the on-call runbook pattern. Includes the staff-level synthesis: designing observability before coding the system.

title: Real-Time OLAP: Druid, ClickHouse, and Pinot at Scale
snippet: 35 minReal-Time OLAP: Druid, ClickHouse, and Pinot at ScaleReal-time OLAP system design deep-dive for sub-second analytics at 1B+ events/day. Covers Druid's tiered segment storage, ClickHouse's MergeTree, and Pinot's star-tree index — including rollup tradeoffs that lock your schema, scatter-gather routing, and distributed systems failure modes most candidates miss.

title: Search Internals: Inverted Index, TF-IDF, Elasticsearch & Ranking
snippet: 24 minSearch Internals: Inverted Index, TF-IDF, Elasticsearch & RankingFull-text search powers every application. Master the inverted index data structure, TF-IDF relevance scoring, BM25 (the modern standard), Elasticsearch's distributed shard architecture, query execution pipeline, and the tradeoffs between exact-match, fuzzy, and semantic search.

title: Stream Processing Systems: Flink, Kafka Streams, Windows, and Exactly-Once
snippet: 34 minStream Processing Systems: Flink, Kafka Streams, Windows, and Exactly-OnceA system design deep dive on real-time stream processing architecture. Learn how to choose Flink vs Kafka Streams, design windowed aggregations, handle late data, and implement exactly-once semantics with production-grade failure recovery.

title: Zero-Downtime Deployments: Blue-Green, Canary, Rolling, and Feature Flags
snippet: Deployment strategy is a system design decision with direct reliability consequences. Covers blue-green, canary (with statistical analysis), rolling updates, and feature flags — including the database migration compatibility problem that trips up most E5 candidates.

title: UML Class Diagrams: Notation, Relationships, Mermaid Syntax
snippet: UML class diagrams are the visual language of LLD interviews, interviewers expect you to draw them on a whiteboard. Learn class notation, the six relationships (inheritance, realization, composition, aggregation, association, dependency), multiplicity, and Mermaid classDiagram syntax with Parking Lot examples.

title: LLD Case Study: Thread-Safe LRU Cache
snippet: Design an O(1) LRU cache using HashMap + doubly linked list. Covers the exact data structure invariants, thread-safe implementation with per-key segmented locking, TTL eviction, singleflight to prevent thundering herd, and the step-by-step path from a single-server cache to a distributed Memcached-style tier. The canonical LLD problem that separates engineers who understand data structure composition from those who just memorize it.

title: LLD Case Study: Extensible Rate Limiter
snippet: Design a thread-safe, extensible rate limiter supporting token bucket and sliding window log algorithms, with clean interfaces, deterministic testability, and a clear migration path from a single-process limiter to a distributed Redis-backed cluster. Covers the exact concurrency pitfalls, race conditions in read-modify-write, thundering-herd on cold buckets, and how production systems like Stripe, Cloudflare, and Kong solve them.

title: LLD Interview Framework & Walkthrough
snippet: A repeatable 5-step framework for LLD interviews. Includes a complete walkthrough of the Parking Lot problem, the most commonly asked LLD problem, with class diagrams and code.

title: How to Approach a Low Level Design Interview
snippet: The mindset and communication playbook for low level design interviews. Covers what interviewers grade on (SOLID adherence, design patterns, extensibility), time budgeting, recovery patterns, and the failure modes that catch senior candidates in object-oriented design and lld rounds.

title: How to Design at LLD: Blank Whiteboard to Class Diagram
snippet: 48 minHow to Design at LLD: Blank Whiteboard to Class DiagramThe mechanical playbook for low level design interview execution. Covers entity extraction, SOLID principles, picking the right design patterns (Strategy, State, Observer, Builder), concurrency primitives, dependency injection, and canonical lld problems like parking lot and library as object-oriented case studies.

title: Behavioral Patterns: Observer, Command, Strategy, Template Method & Chain of Responsibility
snippet: Behavioral design patterns define how objects communicate and delegate work. Master Observer (event notification), Command (encapsulate requests), Strategy (interchangeable algorithms), Template Method (algorithm skeleton with extension points), and Chain of Responsibility (pipeline processing), with Java examples and real interview applications.

title: Design Patterns for LLD Interviews
snippet: The 7 most frequently tested design patterns in LLD interviews with concrete code examples and the exact problems they solve. Know which pattern to reach for and why.

title: Refactoring Patterns: Strangler Fig, Extract Class, Anti-Corruption Layer & Legacy Modernization
snippet: The production refactoring toolkit for senior engineers: how to safely modernize legacy codebases without a big-bang rewrite. Covers Strangler Fig migration, Extract Class/Interface, Introduce Parameter Object, Anti-Corruption Layer, and the Branch-by-Abstraction technique with concrete Java/Python examples.

title: Structural Patterns: Proxy, Adapter, Facade, Composite & Decorator
snippet: 26 minStructural Patterns: Proxy, Adapter, Facade, Composite & DecoratorStructural design patterns define how classes and objects are composed into larger structures. Master Proxy (lazy loading, access control, caching), Adapter (interface translation), Facade (simplified subsystem interface), Composite (tree structures), and Decorator (dynamic behavior extension), each with Java examples and real interview applications.

title: LLD Anti-Patterns: God Class, Anemic Models, Primitive Obsession
snippet: Anti-pattern recognition is the strongest signal of senior-level OOP fluency. Covers the six most-tested LLD anti-patterns, God Class, Anemic Domain Model, Primitive Obsession, Feature Envy, Shotgun Surgery, Circular Dependencies, with violation/fix code and the interviewer probes that reveal them.

title: API Design Principles: REST, Idempotency, Versioning, and Error Contracts
snippet: Well-designed APIs are force multipliers, enabling teams to work independently and systems to evolve safely. Master REST's UNIFORM INTERFACE constraint, idempotency keys, API versioning strategies, and error contracts, the LLD interview topics that separate senior from junior engineers at FAANG.

title: Database Schema Design for LLD: ER Modeling, Indexing, and Schema Evolution
snippet: Amazon and Google LLD interviews pivot from class diagrams to schema design within 20 minutes. Master ER modeling, normalization tradeoffs, B-tree indexing strategy, and online migrations, the topics every candidate hits but few prep with the depth FAANG expects.

title: SOLID Principles in Practice
snippet: 20 minSOLID Principles in PracticeSOLID principles aren't just theory, they're the vocabulary interviewers use to evaluate your OOP design. Learn each principle with concrete before/after code examples and how to spot violations.

title: LLD Case Study: Airline Reservation & Seating (PNR, Segments, Overbooking Policy)
snippet: 27 minLLD Case Study: Airline Reservation & Seating (PNR, Segments, Overbooking Policy)LLD for airline reservations: PNR, segments per FlightInstance, seat holds, FareRule/Strategy, overbooking policy, and TOCTOU-safe seat commit. Ties to hotel/movie-ticket holds; names DCS check-in and standby as extensions.

title: LLD Case Study: ATM Machine System Design
snippet: 39 minLLD Case Study: ATM Machine System DesignDesign an ATM system covering hardware abstraction, session state machines, security constraints, and cash dispensing algorithms. Covers the exact questions FAANG interviewers probe: preventing double-dispensing, handling mid-transaction cash exhaustion, and why concurrency must be handled at the bank service layer.

title: LLD Case Study: Chess Game System Design
snippet: 36 minLLD Case Study: Chess Game System DesignDesign a fully-featured chess engine covering piece hierarchy, move validation, check/checkmate detection, and special moves. Covers the exact tradeoffs interviewers probe at senior level: where move validation lives, how undo works, and how to extend the system for AI opponents without rewriting the core.

title: LLD Case Study: Elevator System (Scheduling, State Machines, Concurrency)
snippet: 45 minLLD Case Study: Elevator System (Scheduling, State Machines, Concurrency)Production-grade object-oriented elevator system design covering SCAN scheduling, state machine modeling, multi-elevator coordination, and failure modes. Covers what most prep resources get wrong: the difference between SCAN and LOOK, and why nearest-car is correct for modern buildings.

title: LLD Case Study: Food Delivery System
snippet: 33 minLLD Case Study: Food Delivery SystemDesign the domain model and class hierarchy for a food delivery platform like DoorDash, Uber Eats, or Swiggy. Covers order state machine, Observer-driven notifications, Strategy-based pricing, SOLID principles, thread-safe concurrent order placement, and extensibility for scheduled orders and group orders.

title: LLD Case Study: Hotel Booking System
snippet: 34 minLLD Case Study: Hotel Booking SystemDesign a hotel booking system using object-oriented design patterns, State machine for booking lifecycle, Strategy for dynamic pricing, and optimistic locking to prevent double-booking. Covers availability modeling, cancellation policies, and chain-scale extension.

title: LLD Case Study: Library Management System
snippet: 31 minLLD Case Study: Library Management SystemDesign a Library Management System using object-oriented design patterns, Strategy for search, Observer for reservations, and per-copy locking for concurrency. The Book vs BookItem distinction is the #1 mistake candidates make and what senior engineers probe for.

title: LLD Case Study: Meeting Room Scheduler (Overlap Detection, Booking States, Concurrency)
snippet: 25 minLLD Case Study: Meeting Room Scheduler (Overlap Detection, Booking States, Concurrency)Production-grade LLD for meeting room scheduling with interval overlap checks, tentative-to-confirmed booking lifecycle, optimistic locking for double-booking prevention, and observer-driven attendee notifications.

title: LLD Case Study: Parking Lot System (Production-Grade)
snippet: Deep-dive production design of a multi-floor parking lot with EV charging, dynamic pricing, reservations, and concurrent access. Goes beyond basic OOP to cover lock granularity, optimistic concurrency, and real extension patterns interviewers probe at senior level.

title: LLD Case Study: Ride-Sharing Booking Engine
snippet: 32 minLLD Case Study: Ride-Sharing Booking EngineObject-oriented design of the Uber/Lyft booking and matching engine, entities, state machines, fare calculation, and concurrency. Focused on LLD class design, not distributed architecture.

title: LLD Case Study: Online Shopping Cart & Checkout (Lines, Idempotency, Order Boundaries)
snippet: Object-oriented design for a production-style cart: immutable product snapshots, merge-on-login, promotion strategies, and checkout with idempotency keys aligned to payment APIs. Explains when cart inventory is advisory vs when it must call reservation services, and how Order differs from Cart as an aggregate.

title: LLD Case Study: Social Follow & Home Feed (Graph, Push vs Pull, Feed Strategies)
snippet: 26 minLLD Case Study: Social Follow & Home Feed (Graph, Push vs Pull, Feed Strategies)Object-oriented design for follow relationships, optional follow requests, blocking, and feed generation. Teaches how to model the social graph separate from posts, when to use Strategy for push-fanout vs pull-merge feeds, and what to leave to infrastructure without faking a single Map that scales to billions of edges.

title: LLD Case Study: Task & Job Scheduler (Cron, Queues, At-Least-Once, Idempotent Workers)
snippet: 29 minLLD Case Study: Task & Job Scheduler (Cron, Queues, At-Least-Once, Idempotent Workers)LLD for schedulable jobs: cron/one-shot triggers, Run state machine, worker claim+lease, exponential backoff, DLQ, and at-least-once delivery. Aligns with Conductor/Sidekiq-style idempotent handlers vs fake exactly-once.

title: LLD Case Study: Movie & Event Ticket Booking (Seat Map, Holds, Concurrency)
snippet: 30 minLLD Case Study: Movie & Event Ticket Booking (Seat Map, Holds, Concurrency)Object-oriented design for BookMyShow-style ticketing: venue seat graph, per-show instance inventory, time-boxed seat holds, and TOCTOU-safe booking. Covers pricing strategies, idempotent checkout, and extension to best-available search without an HLD detour.

title: LLD Case Study: Vending Machine
snippet: 31 minLLD Case Study: Vending MachineDesign a production-grade vending machine with multi-payment support, change dispensing, and inventory management. Covers State, Strategy, and Observer patterns applied to a canonical LLD interview problem.

title: Python asyncio: Event Loop, async/await, and Production-Safe Concurrent I/O
snippet: 50 minPython asyncio: Event Loop, async/await, and Production-Safe Concurrent I/OInterview-grade asyncio for backend engineers: how the event loop schedules coroutines, why the GIL still matters, asyncio.Lock vs threading.Lock, structured concurrency with TaskGroup, avoiding blocked loops, and when to bail out to threads or processes. Maps cleanly to FastAPI, aiohttp, and async database drivers.

title: Concurrency Models Compared: Threads, asyncio, Actor Runtime, and CSP
snippet: 44 minConcurrency Models Compared: Threads, asyncio, Actor Runtime, and CSPStaff-level LLD framing: map workloads to OS preemptive threads, cooperative asyncio, Erlang-style actors, Go channels/CSP, and fork-join pools, with explicit failure modes (deadlock vs starvation vs head-of-line blocking) and when each model loses to plain sequential code.

title: Concurrency Patterns: Thread Safety, Producer-Consumer, Read-Write Lock & Thread Pool
snippet: 27 minConcurrency Patterns: Thread Safety, Producer-Consumer, Read-Write Lock & Thread PoolThread safety and concurrent design are tested at every senior+ interview. Master the fundamentals of mutual exclusion (ReentrantLock, synchronized), the producer-consumer pattern with blocking queues, read-write locks for read-heavy systems, thread-safe singletons, and thread pool design, all with Java examples and Python equivalents.

title: Thread Pool Pattern: Fixed, Cached, Scheduled, and Work-Stealing Executors
snippet: 23 minThread Pool Pattern: Fixed, Cached, Scheduled, and Work-Stealing ExecutorsThread pools are the primary concurrency primitive in production Java and Python systems, they prevent thread explosion, manage CPU resources, and control backpressure. This guide covers thread pool mechanics, the four Java ExecutorService variants, rejection policies, work-stealing queues (ForkJoinPool), and the deadlock patterns that trap candidates at Meta, Google, and Amazon LLD rounds.

title: AI-Assisted Development and Vibe Coding: Fast Output Without Quality Collapse
snippet: A practical framework for using AI coding tools in production teams without creating hidden technical debt. Covers prompt-to-PR workflow, verification gates, security constraints, architecture guardrails, and the difference between useful vibe coding and irresponsible automation.

title: Code Review Excellence: The Craft That Most Engineers Never Learn
snippet: A concrete methodology for giving and receiving code review that improves code quality, accelerates team growth, and does not create adversarial dynamics. Covers the review hierarchy (correctness before style), how to write feedback that gets implemented, the approve-vs-block decision framework, reviewing for future maintainers, and the anti-patterns, LGTM culture, nitpick spirals, ego-driven rejections, that poison engineering team velocity.

title: What Good Code Actually Looks Like: Engineering Craft Beyond the Linter
snippet: 37 minWhat Good Code Actually Looks Like: Engineering Craft Beyond the LinterA practical framework for the six dimensions of code quality that interviewers and senior engineers actually evaluate: correctness, clarity, testability, error handling, performance awareness, and maintainability. Covers naming as design, the rule of three, when to comment and what to say, the hidden cost of cleverness, and the before/after transformations that turn adequate code into production-grade code.

title: GitHub End-to-End Workflow: From Issue to Safe Production Merge
snippet: 17 minGitHub End-to-End Workflow: From Issue to Safe Production MergeA production-grade GitHub workflow for software teams: issue scoping, branch strategy, pull request quality gates, CI policy, review dynamics, and merge/release discipline. Learn what interviewers expect when they ask how you ship safely at speed in modern engineering organizations.

title: Metric Anomaly Triage: Is This a Real Problem or an Instrumentation Bug?
snippet: 25 minMetric Anomaly Triage: Is This a Real Problem or an Instrumentation Bug?Structured methodology for diagnosing sudden metric drops or spikes before escalating. Covers the validation-first approach, driver-tree decomposition, correlated metric analysis, and the discipline of scoping anomalies before jumping to product explanations. Tested at senior and staff IC levels at every major tech company.

title: Performance Profiling & Optimization: Python, JVM, Flame Graphs, and APM
snippet: End-to-end methodology for profiling production services, from Python cProfile and py-spy to JVM Flight Recorder and async-profiler, reading Brendan Gregg flame graphs, and correlating distributed traces in Datadog and OpenTelemetry. Covers CPU-bound vs I/O-bound identification, memory leak diagnosis, and the non-obvious insights that separate senior engineers from candidates who only know the tools exist.

title: Feature Flags: Safe Rollouts, Kill Switches, and the Dark Launch Pattern
snippet: A concrete framework for using feature flags as a production engineering tool, not just a product experimentation mechanism.
